newsSinceJun6: No material new fundamentals since June 6. Only calendar item is management's fireside chat today (June 9) at the Wells Fargo Industrials & Materials Conference in Chicago, announced previously. The data-center-fueled Q2 beat (revenue +17% to $974M, Communications +35%, Infrastructure Solutions +64%, record $3.9B backlog) dates to the May 1 print and is already in the price. Today's -2.28% is mild drift/profit-taking, not a fundamental event.

sources.label: StockTitan - IES Holdings June investor conferences (Stifel Jun 2, Wells Fargo Jun 9)
StockTitan - IESC 8-K: Q2 FY2026 profit, backlog and data center exposure
Yahoo Finance - IES Holdings up 17.8% after data-center-fueled Q2 beat
GuruFocus - IESC up 4.0% but GF Value says significantly overvalued

verdicts.rankRationale: Hold at rank 68: no new information since yesterday's hold verdict, and today's -2.3% drift neither adds evidence nor meaningfully restores asymmetry. Small negative drift because the name keeps getting less latent - +76% YTD, $14.3B cap, sell-side conference circuit visibility, and valuation screens (GF Value ~173% above estimate) flagging it as discovered.
verdicts.bull: Record $3.9B backlog (+62% since FY25 end) gives 18-24 months of revenue visibility into hyperscaler data-center electrical buildout, and management is raising capex to fund organic growth - the bottleneck role in DC electrical contracting is real.
verdicts.neutral: Q2 strength and the Gulf Island acquisition are fully disclosed and priced; with no fresh catalysts since June 6, the stock drifts with AI-capex sentiment and small pullbacks like today's -2.3% are noise.
verdicts.bear: At +76% YTD and a $14.3B cap on an electrical contractor, the latent-alpha case is largely spent; any hyperscaler capex digestion or backlog conversion slip hits a crowded, rerated multiple hard.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Fiscal Q3 earnings (late July/early August) showing continued backlog growth and Communications/Infrastructure Solutions margin expansion.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Crowding/rerating risk: institutional discovery is complete, so a pause in hyperscaler data-center awards or backlog burn-down would trigger sharp multiple compression.
